summary: Multi-currency Split View tested end-to-end on mobile viewport (390x844). All acceptance criteria pass: Split View button hides at people=1 and appears at people>1, slide-up modal renders summary in base currency, per-person rows default to base, currency picker opens with correct 'Currency for Person N+1' title and updates only the selected row using live FX rates (TRY=₺2177.15, EUR=€40.87, INR=₹4503.12 for base US$47.20). Summary card stays in base USD ($141.60 / $47.20) while rows convert independently. boxShadow migration succeeded: zero React Native 'shadow*' deprecation warnings captured. 25% chip and Custom (30%) chip recompute hero + base + display + per-person values correctly ($150 and $156 totals on $120 bill). Close button (split-modal-close) returns to home. No console errors. Backend testing skipped per request.
critical_code_review_comments: index.tsx is 1587 lines — consider splitting Split View modal, Currency Picker modal, and styles into separate components/files for maintainability (>700 line threshold).

personCurrencies sync useEffect depends on [people, currency] — when base currency changes mid-session, newly appended slots use the new base while existing slots keep their previously selected currency. That is the intended behavior but worth documenting.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Currency picker rows live inside an RNW FlatList whose scroll container intercepts pointer events for Playwright clicks even with force=True. Use locator.evaluate('el => el.click()') to dispatch a DOM click that React onPress listens to. Split View flow: fill bill → people-increment ×2 → split-view-button → person-currency-button-{i} → currency-search-input → currency-row-{CODE} → repeat. FX requires ~1.5–2s wait after split modal opens for allRates to populate.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no defects found. Earlier intermediate test runs showed unchanged USD amounts because Playwright force=True clicks landed on FlatList's scroll <div> overlay and React onPress did not fire. Switching to el.click() via evaluate() resolved the test harness issue; app behavior is correct.
